Printing Headers, Footers, and Inline Headers

Printing Headers, Footers, and Inline Headers

You can use header, trailer, and break frames to print titles, column headings, date and time, page numbers, subtotals and totals, or to suppress printing.

Header, trailer and break frames can contain labels to provide fixed text, and non-database fields to which you assign data from variables or occurrence source fields. This enables you to create typical report headers such as:

|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Date: 10 June, 2015 Invoices for Customer: CUSTOMER Page: 6 |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|

where the date and page number are provided by ProcScript functions and the customer name is the customer name from the current occurrence.

The following sections illustrate typical uses of header, trailer, and break frames.

Inserting Page Numbers

Uniface does not automatically produce page numbers because there are too many different design considerations to be taken into account. For example:

  • The first page of a report might not be numbered.
  • You may only want to number even (or odd) pages.
  • You may require extensions to the page number (for example, a department number or name).

Use the $page ProcScript function in the getFocus trigger of a frame to generate page numbers.

; frame : HEADER.USYS
trigger Frame Gets Focus
PAGE.HEADER.USYS = $page

Inserting Inline Headers with Break Frames

You can use inline headers for many kinds of text, such as column titles, warnings about entities with no occurrences, standard text that you want to insert in your report, or language variations of your report.

You can use labels in entities and named frames for text, but using break frames enables you to determine the conditions when the text should be displayed.

Draw the break frame exactly as you would like it to be printed. Use the printbreak statement to print the break frame at the point where it should appear. The printbreak statement is allowed only in the getFocus trigger and the leavePrinted trigger.

Avoiding Unnecessary Repetition

If you know that the same data is going to appear in multiple occurrences of an entity you can significantly improve the clarity and legibility of your report by printing such data only once, when the value changes. Use a break frame for this.

For example, to print an invoice date only when it changes:

  1. Draw a field in a break frame and load the invoice date into it. 
    ; frame : BREAK.USYS
trigger getFocus
DATE.BREAK.USYS = INVDATE.INVOICE
  2. Print the break frame only when the invoice date changes. Enter the following ProcScript code in the getFocus trigger of the INVOICE entity (for example): 
    ; entity : INVOICE
trigger getFocus
compare/previous (DATE) from "INVOICE" ;check if date of previous
  ;invoice is different
if ($result != 1) ;previous date is different, or
  ;no previous invoice
 if ($curocc(INVOICE) > 1) ;and if this is not the first invoice
 eject ;force page break for new invoice date
 endif
 printbreak "BREAK.USYS" ;print break frame BREAK.USYS for
  ;column titles and invoice date
endif

Maintaining Running Totals

You can print running totals based on values in printed data. To do so you need to create a field in the desired frame and initialize or reset its value with a calculated value from a variable or non-database field.

If you are using global variables or general variables ($1 through $99), and they should be empty before you print your report, set these variables to 0 in the exec operation of the component.

Depending on the sort of calculation you are performing (day totals, page totals, grand totals, and so on) you may need to reset the field in the frame to zero in the course of running the component, regardless of the type of variable you are using. Use the entity's getFocus and leavePrinted triggers to add each source value to the destination and (if relevant) for resetting the destination to zero.

Use the break frame's getFocus trigger for loading values from a temporary destination (such as a variable) into a field in a break frame.

Example: Printing Totals and Subtotals

Given an outer entity of CUSTOMER and an inner entity of INVOICE, print a subtotal of all invoices for the day each time the date changes:

  1. Draw a break frame named MONTH_BREAK.USYS to print the total amount of all invoices for the customer for a month. Inside the frame, draw a non-database field SUBTOTAL with the same layout and interface definitions as the source field AMOUNT.INVOICE.
  2. In the getFocus trigger of the outer entity CUSTOMER, reset the counter by clearing the field in the break frame. 
    ; entity : CUSTOMER
trigger getFocus
SUBTOTAL.MONTH_BREAK.USYS = ""
end
  3. In the getFocus trigger of the inner entity INVOICE, add the source value (AMOUNT) to the value in the break frame field SUBTOTAL.MONTH_BREAK.USYS for each occurrence of the inner entity. 
    ; entity : INVOICE
 trigger getFocus
SUBTOTAL.MONTH_BREAK = SUBTOTAL.MONTH_BREAK + AMOUNT.INVOICE
end
    Note: If you wanted to calculate the total of all invoices for the customer, you would use the leavePrinted trigger.

  4. Define the condition when the break frame should be printed (in this case, the change of invoice date) and use the printbreak command to print the break frame. If necessary, reset the field after the break frame is printed.

    ; entity : INVOICE
trigger leavePrinted
compare/next (DATE) from "INVOICE" ;check if the date of the next
  ;invoice is different
if ($result != 1) ;if the next date is different or
  ;there is no next invoice
 printbreak "MONTH_BREAK.USYS" ;print the break frame MONTH_BREAK
 SUBTOTAL.MONTH_BREAK = ""
endif
end

Example: Defining Headers and Column Headers

In a report about customers and invoices, print the name of the current customer in the header of all pages following the first instance of that customer in the report, and a print column titles for the invoice data.

  1. Draw header frame and a field in the header frame, and—if you want—some text next to it to describe the contents of the field; for example, you could use the text Invoice report for:
  2. In the getFocus trigger of the CUSTOMER entity, which assigns data to a variable: 
    trigger getFocus ; of entity CUSTOMER
 $NAME$ = NAME.CUSTOMER ;store customer name in $NAME$
end
  3. To prevent the name being carried over for the next occurrence of the CUSTOMER entity (because the next occurrence will have a different name), enter ProcScript in the leavePrinted trigger of the entity to clear the variable: 
    trigger leavePrinted ; of entity CUSTOMER
 $NAME$ = "" ;clear this variable in readiness for next customer
end; leavePrinted
  4. To load the customer name into the field (NAME.HEADER.USYS), enter the following ProcScript in the getFocus trigger of the header frame: 
    trigger getFocus ; of frame HEADER.USYS
if ($page = 1) ;on first page
 NAME.HEADER.USYS = "All Customers" ;general title
else
 if ($NAME$ = "") ;first page for a particular customer
 ;LPO of previous customer cleared $name$
 NAME.HEADER.USYS = "..." ;name appears below, in CUSTOMER entity
 else
 NAME.HEADER.USYS = $NAME$ ;print name of current customer
 endif
endif
end; getFocus
  5. Draw a break frame INV_TITLE, and in the frame, a field and column titles of the INVOICE entity. The text must appear in exactly the right position.
  6. At the desired point in the print process, enter a printbreak statement to print the INV_TITLE break frame. 
    trigger getFocus ; of entity INVOICE
compare/previous "INVOICE" ;check that this is the first occurrence (no previous invoice)
if ($result != 1) :if so
 printbreak "INV_TITLE.USYS" ;print the break frame INV_TITLE.USYS
endif
end ; getFocus
Related Links
  • Define Header, Footer, and Break Frames

    To add information at the top and the bottom of printed page, define a header frame and a footer frame in a report (or a form used for printing). To conditionally include information in a report, define break frames.
